task_completion.description: Set the observer location to Saturn in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium bottom information bar or the Location window (F6) clearly shows 'Saturn' as the current observer location.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to July 19, 2013, at 21:27 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium bottom information bar or the Date/Time window (F5) shows the date as 2013-07-19 and the time as 21:27 (or very close to it).

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and ground/landscape, and enable planet labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a black space background with no ground/landscape or atmosphere visible. Planet labels are enabled, showing text names next to celestial bodies.

task_completion.description: Locate and center the camera on Earth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Earth is visible in the Stellarium sky view and is centered on the screen, typically indicated by a selection reticle (crosshairs) and the label 'Earth'.
The Stellarium Configuration window (F2) is open with the 'Save settings' button clicked, or a notification indicates that the settings were saved.

task_completion.description: Take a screenshot of the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A notification in Stellarium indicates a screenshot was saved, or a file manager window shows a new image file in the /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium directory.

task_completion.description: Write exhibit draft notes with required keywords to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation of '/home/ga/Desktop/cassini_exhibit.txt' containing the exact phrases 'The Day the Earth Smiled', 'Saturn', 'Earth', and '2013'.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with Stellarium to set up the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating Stellarium's GUI (Location, Date/Time, Search windows) rather than just opening an existing image or faking the view.

integrity.description: The exhibit notes were constructed naturally.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file contains a coherent draft note, rather than just a direct copy-paste of the JSON prompt or task description.

integrity.description: The screenshot was actually saved to the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot is saved as a file in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium, not just captured to the clipboard without saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The observer planet must be Saturn. The target to center on is Earth. The date must be set to 2013-07-19. Display flags for atmosphere and landscape must be disabled. The exhibit notes must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/cassini_exhibit.txt and contain the exact keywords: 'The Day the Earth Smiled', 'Saturn', 'Earth', '2013'. At least 1 screenshot must be taken and saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium.
